transcription: Patient is a 58-year-old male presenting with stable angina. After evaluation, we recommend starting atorvastatin and scheduling a coronary angiography to assess arterial blockage.
A 34-year-old female presents with a displaced fracture of the right radius after a fall. Recommended treatment is closed reduction and cast immobilization for six weeks.
The patient, a 62-year-old woman, has poorly controlled type 2 diabetes mellitus. We recommend initiating metformin therapy along with dietary counseling and quarterly HbA1c monitoring.
This 45-year-old male smoker reports chronic cough and dyspnea. Spirometry confirms moderate COPD. Recommended treatment includes a long-acting bronchodilator inhaler and smoking cessation program.
A 27-year-old female presents with moderate plaque psoriasis on elbows and knees. Recommended treatment is topical corticosteroids with follow-up in eight weeks to consider phototherapy.
